feature: Delete post for logged in users
description: Allow logged-in users to delete their own posts. The delete button should only be visible to the post author, and the backend should verify ownership before allowing deletion.

tasks.task: Secure the DELETE /posts/:id endpoint in blog.controller.ts. Add authorization header handling to extract the user from the Bearer token using AuthService.validateToken(). Verify that the requesting user's id matches the post's authorId before allowing deletion. Return 401 Unauthorized if no valid token is provided, and 403 Forbidden if the user doesn't own the post. Update the BlogService.delete() method to accept the requesting userId and perform the ownership check, throwing ForbiddenException if unauthorized. Import ForbiddenException from @nestjs/common.

Update the delete functionality to work only for post owners. In App.tsx, pass the 'user' state to the PostDetail component. In PostDetail.tsx, add a 'user' prop (type User | null from ../types), and conditionally render the delete button only when user is logged in and user.id matches post.authorId. Update the handleDelete function to include the Authorization header with the Bearer token from localStorage.getItem('token'). Keep the existing confirmation dialog flow.

example_backend: DELETE /posts/:id without auth header returns 401 Unauthorized
DELETE /posts/:id with invalid token returns 401 Unauthorized
DELETE /posts/:id with valid token but wrong author returns 403 Forbidden
DELETE /posts/:id with valid token and correct author successfully deletes the post
example_frontend: Delete button is NOT visible when user is not logged in
Delete button is NOT visible when logged in user is not the post author
Delete button IS visible when logged in user is the post author
Confirming delete sends request with Authorization header
After successful delete, user is redirected to post list
